recruiter.rationale: recruiter lens defaulted from role-family priors because upstream evidence was thin.

hiring_manager.rationale: hiring_manager lens defaulted from role-family priors because upstream evidence was thin.

peer_technical.rationale: peer_technical lens defaulted from role-family priors because upstream evidence was thin.

document_expectations.rationale: Candidate-agnostic thesis defaulted from architecture_first role-family priors with ai_intensity=adjacent.

confidence.basis: Role-family priors plus available evaluator and research signals.
confidence.evidence_summary: Role-family priors plus available evaluator and research signals.

evidence.claim: Default document thesis is derived from classification, stakeholder surface, and ATS posture priors.

section_emphasis.rationale: Summary should establish role-fit and proof posture quickly.
Key achievements carry the condensed proof ladder.
Experience is the main proof surface for this role class.
Competencies support ATS parseability without dominating the document.
AI highlights are visible when the AI intensity warrants explicit signaling.

cv_shape_expectations.rationale: Concrete CV shape mirrors the document thesis and conservative ATS posture priors.

confidence.basis: Role-family priors plus thesis alignment.

evidence.claim: Default CV shape is derived from document thesis, AI intensity, and ATS posture priors.

ideal_candidate_presentation_model.visible_identity: Principal platform architect who improves developer systems and safely enables AI-assisted workflows.

must_signal.rationale: architecture judgment is part of the default framing for this role family.
ownership scope is part of the default framing for this role family.
production impact is part of the default framing for this role family.

should_signal.rationale: ai depth is useful but secondary for this evaluator surface.
platform reliability is useful but secondary for this evaluator surface.
stakeholder alignment is useful but secondary for this evaluator surface.

de_emphasize.rationale: tool listing should not outrun stronger proof.
generic leadership claim should not outrun stronger proof.

proof_ladder.rationale: Lead with architecture proof because it is upstream-prioritized for this role.
Lead with metric proof because it is upstream-prioritized for this role.
Lead with stakeholder proof because it is upstream-prioritized for this role.

credibility_markers.rationale: architecture proof should be concrete and recruiter-visible.
metric proof should be concrete and recruiter-visible.
stakeholder proof should be concrete and recruiter-visible.

risk_flags.rationale: Avoid unsupported AI positioning that outruns the proof ladder.
Do not let tool lists displace scope and production evidence.

recruiter.rationale: recruiter framing defaulted from stakeholder coverage and role-family priors.

hiring_manager.rationale: hiring_manager framing defaulted from stakeholder coverage and role-family priors.

peer_technical.rationale: peer_technical framing defaulted from stakeholder coverage and role-family priors.

confidence.basis: Ideal-candidate framing defaulted from upstream role-family, proof-map, and stakeholder priors.
confidence.evidence_summary: Ideal-candidate framing defaulted from upstream role-family, proof-map, and stakeholder priors.

experience_dimension_weights.rationale: Experience-dimension salience defaulted from role-family priors plus available stakeholder and proof-map pressure.

confidence.basis: Deterministic role-family priors with stakeholder and proof-map pressure.

evidence.claim: Default weight priors are grounded in role family, stakeholder coverage, and proof-map pressure.

global_rules.condition: Leadership scope evidence is limited by seniority or direct-report envelope.
global_rules.action: Cap leadership framing to the evidence band and require direct proof for stronger leadership claims.
global_rules.basis: Leadership claims must not outrun the leadership evidence envelope.

confidence.basis: Leadership cap derived from seniority, direct reports, and 4.2.5 leadership band.
confidence.evidence_summary: Leadership cap derived from seniority, direct reports, and 4.2.5 leadership band.

experience.condition: Every high-emphasis claim needs at least one credibility marker.
experience.action: Require named systems, scoped metrics, or ownership markers before emphasis is increased.
experience.basis: Emphasis must degrade gracefully when proof is thin.

confidence.basis: Credibility ladder rules are anchored to the proof ladder and evidence map.
confidence.evidence_summary: Credibility ladder rules are anchored to the proof ladder and evidence map.

allowed_if_evidenced.condition: Architecture framing is grounded in upstream mandate and proof.
allowed_if_evidenced.action: Architecture claims are permitted when anchored to direct proof.
allowed_if_evidenced.basis: Architecture claim posture must track the architecture evidence band.

confidence.basis: Role mandate and proof map define the architecture claim band.
confidence.evidence_summary: Role mandate and proof map define the architecture claim band.

allowed_if_evidenced.condition: metric claims stay optional until direct proof is available.
allowed_if_evidenced.action: Allow this proof category only when direct evidence is visible and truthful.
allowed_if_evidenced.basis: Should-signal proof categories need an explicit evidentiary escape hatch.

confidence.basis: Should-signal proof categories remain conditional on direct proof.
confidence.evidence_summary: Should-signal proof categories remain conditional on direct proof.

downgrade_rules.condition: Stakeholder-management evidence is indirect or inferred.
downgrade_rules.action: Use conservative stakeholder-language unless direct cross-functional proof is visible.
downgrade_rules.basis: Stakeholder claims should not outrun the evaluator surface.

confidence.basis: Stakeholder surface and proof map determine safe stakeholder framing.
confidence.evidence_summary: Stakeholder surface and proof map determine safe stakeholder framing.

downgrade_rules.condition: tool_listing should stay secondary unless direct proof is strong.
downgrade_rules.action: Prefer softened wording or omit the claim when supporting proof is thin.
downgrade_rules.basis: 4.2.4 de-emphasize signals must remain visible in claim-policy rules.

confidence.basis: De-emphasize priors should degrade unsupported proof-led framing.
confidence.evidence_summary: De-emphasize priors should degrade unsupported proof-led framing.

omit_rules.condition: Requested title exceeds acceptable_titles or title_strategy envelope.
omit_rules.action: Do not authorize titles outside acceptable_titles; use closest truthful framing.
omit_rules.basis: Title claims must stay within the truthful title envelope.

confidence.basis: Peer title strategy and acceptable_titles define the safe envelope.
confidence.evidence_summary: Peer title strategy and acceptable_titles define the safe envelope.

omit_rules.condition: AI depth is weak, adjacent, or absent in upstream evidence.
omit_rules.action: Do not lead the summary with AI depth claims without direct proof.
omit_rules.basis: AI claim policy must track classification intensity and 4.2.5 caps.

confidence.basis: Classification AI intensity plus dimension-weight cap defines the AI claim envelope.
confidence.evidence_summary: Classification AI intensity plus dimension-weight cap defines the AI claim envelope.

omit_rules.condition: AI highlights would overstate AI depth for adjacent or non-AI roles.
omit_rules.action: Do not surface a dedicated AI highlights section without direct proof.
omit_rules.basis: Low-AI roles must fail closed on dedicated AI depth framing.

confidence.basis: AI section policy and intensity define whether a dedicated AI section is safe.
confidence.evidence_summary: AI section policy and intensity define whether a dedicated AI section is safe.

omit_rules.condition: Domain expertise is not directly evidenced beyond the JD context.
omit_rules.action: Do not infer deep domain expertise from job fit alone.
omit_rules.basis: Domain claims require more than JD-only inference.

confidence.basis: Domain claims need explicit upstream evidence beyond the JD.
confidence.evidence_summary: Domain claims need explicit upstream evidence beyond the JD.

omit_rules.condition: Metric or scale proof is weak, generic, or lacks scope.
omit_rules.action: Omit metric-led claims until direct scope and scale proof exists.
omit_rules.basis: Fabricated metrics and scale are not permitted.

confidence.basis: Proof-map quality and anti-pattern priors define metric safety.
confidence.evidence_summary: Proof-map quality and anti-pattern priors define metric safety.

forbidden_claim_patterns.reason: Reject inflated performance language that implies unsupported superiority.

confidence.basis: Buzzword-stacking anti-pattern prior.

forbidden_claim_patterns.reason: Reject inflated leadership wording without direct proof.

confidence.basis: Leadership-overclaim anti-pattern prior.

forbidden_claim_patterns.reason: Reject status language that implies external authority without evidence.

confidence.basis: External-authority anti-pattern prior.

credibility_ladder_rules.rationale: Degrade emphasis from strongest proof to safer adjacent proof when evidence is thin.

confidence.basis: Role-family credibility ladder prior.

truth_constrained_emphasis_rules.rationale: Truth-constrained emphasis rules defaulted deterministically from peer-owned presentation contracts and evidence envelopes.

confidence.basis: Deterministic emphasis-rule priors built from peer contracts and upstream envelopes.
confidence.evidence_summary: Deterministic emphasis-rule priors built from peer contracts and upstream envelopes.

evidence.claim: Emphasis rules are bounded by title, AI, leadership, architecture, and proof envelopes from the parent presentation contract.

notes: Rules describe claim policy only, never candidate truth or CV prose.

reject_signals: AI hype without shipped platform evidence.

unresolved_questions: truth_constrained_emphasis_rules used deterministic defaults for some claim-policy decisions.
notes: presentation_contract now ships 4.2.2 document/shape, 4.2.4 ideal-candidate framing, 4.2.5 experience-dimension weights, and 4.2.6 truth-constrained emphasis rules.
